messages.content:
Which is true of chlorophyll?

Accessory pigments convert chlorophyll into energy


Chlorophyll absorbs all visible light energy


Chlorophyll becomes oxidized by light energy

 

Accessory pigments reduce chlorophyll


Chlorophyll is reduced by red wavelengths
Chlorophyll is reduced by red wavelengths.

What color of visible light does chlorophyll reflect?

Violet


Blue

 

Green


Red
Chlorophyll reflects green light.

When chlorophyll absorbs light energy, this causes its electrons to move...

to a negative charge


to a ground state


to an excited state

 

to another chlorophyll molecule


to a neutral charge
When chlorophyll absorbs light energy, this causes its electrons to move to an excited state.

Which of the following is a reactant of photosynthesis and a product of cellular respiration?

oxygen


carbon dioxide

 

glucose


light energy
The reactant of photosynthesis and a product of cellular respiration is oxygen.

The color of a pigment is the color wavelength that is

reflected


lowest in energy


highest in energy

 

oxidized


absorbed
The color of a pigment is the color wavelength that is reflected.

What is produced from the light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis that are used in the dark reactions?

NADPH, ATP, and Oxygen


Water and Sunlight


NADPH and ATP

 

Water, Sunlight, and Carbon dioxide


Electrons and Protons
NADPH and ATP are produced from the light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis, and they are used in the dark reactions.

Electrons are excited in what molecule of the reaction center?

Electron transport chain


Primary electron acceptor


Chlorophyll a

 

Water


Accessory pigments
Electrons are excited in chlorophyll a, which is the molecule found in the reaction center.

What is the relationship between NADP+ and NADPH in the light reaction?

NADP+ is oxidized to NADPH


NADPH is oxidized to NADP+


NADPH is reduced to NADP+

 

NADP+ is reduced to NADPH


NADP+ is broken into NADP + H+
NADP+ is reduced to NADPH in the light reaction.

ATP is created during the light reactions of photosynthesis in a manner that is similar to cellular respiration. How is this accomplished?

by the citric acid cycle and NAD+ reduction


by the energy derived from photosystems I and II


by the creation of a water molecule by joining protons and electrons

 

by a proton concentration gradient and ATP synthase


by the splitting of a water molecule and movement of electrons through ATP synthase
ATP is created during the light reactions of photosynthesis by a proton concentration gradient and ATP synthase.

Electrons are excited by energy absorbed by

the thylakoid membrane


oxygen


water

 

proteins in the reaction center


chlorophyll and accessory pigments
Electrons are excited by energy absorbed by chlorophyll and accessory pigments.

How many of the G3P molecules that exit the Calvin Cycle are used to form glucose?

Two molecules of G3P


One molecule of G3P

 

Five molecules of G3P


Six molecules of G3P
Two molecules of G3P that exit the Calvin Cycle are used to form glucose.

Which is true of the dark reactions?

They do not directly use sunlight energy


They use sunlight


They occur only at night

 

Energy is derived from food to fuel the reactions


They create products that are light-sensitive
The dark reactions, also known as the Calvin Cycle, do not directly use sunlight energy.

Which of the following are produced in the dark reactions?

ADP, NADP+, G3P, Carbon dioxide


ADP, NADP+, G3P, Oxygen


ADP, NADP+, G3P

 

ATP, NADPH, Glucose, Oxygen


ATP, NADPH, Oxygen, G3P
ADP, NADP+, and G3P are produced in the dark reactions (Calvin Cycle).

Which of the following are used in the dark reactions but produced in the light reactions?

ADP, NADP+


ATP, NADPH


Oxygen, NADPH

 

Carbon dioxide, ATP


RuBP, ATP, NADPH
ATP and NADPH are produced in the light reactions and used in the dark reactions.

Which describes carbon fixation in the dark reactions?

RuBP is converted into carbon dioxide


Three carbon dioxide molecules are directly rearranged and energized into G3P


Atmospheric oxygen is converted into carbon dioxide

 

RuBisCo converts inorganic carbon dioxide into organic molecules


Phosphate groups are donated by ATP to 3-carbon molecules
RuBisCo converts inorganic carbon dioxide into organic molecules in the process of carbon fixation in the dark reactions.

Early Romans could MOST likely be said to have an _____ view on religion?

faithful


disciplined

 

strict


open-minded
Early Romans could most likely be said to have a disciplined view on religion.
